title_guess: South of England: Standen, Capel, Petworth, Churt, Ash Vale

pages.text:
us on Sunday. We discussed Annette's care while Simon went on a bike ride. After Sunday lunch we head towards Richard Holroyd in Cambridge. He was not in. So we made a cup of tea & drove home.

__Saturday June 15th__
9.40. we leave for the south of England. We visit Standen, the Beale family home, a lovely Arts & Crafts house 1880. The interior is furnished as if from 1920. Mrs. Beale & 2 of her daughters sewed magnificent wall hangings, bedspreads, cushions & chair seats. In the dining room

some of the chairs have a mythical beast in very small tapestry in the middle, sewn by the Royal College of Needlework & the Beale women sewed the surroundings. Philip Webb designed the house, Morris & Co did many of the textiles, William de Morgan ceramics. The bedroom has really lovely Gimson furniture, 4 or 5 pieces. [?Voyag-] Nice grounds. We got lost in the woods & nearly arrived late at Janny's mother's house. §Sonja lives in a big house at Capel in a huge house divided into 14 flats.
	There's a swimming pool & tennis court. lovely big airey rooms & views of woods
R & L [box]   We had a good meal & slept [?counter] in the van on the drive.
	__Sunday__ we went to Mass with Sonja St. John's Horsham. Great noisy children at the back, a nice service.
	Off to __Partridge Green__ where we are on a __Farm__ *** with easy cycling onto the Downs Link cycle path.

pages.sketch_desc: Glued-in printed map showing a terrain cross-section of the Downs Link route, with place labels from west to east: Shoreham, South Downs, Clay Vale, Greenwood Rdge, Southwater, [illegible], Cowfold, High Weald, North Downs, Guildford. A dashed line marks the cycle route. Handwritten annotation reads 'we cycled 10k & 10k back'. Printed caption reads '— Route of the Downs Link'.
pages.text:
[glued-in map: Route of the Downs Link, showing hills/terrain cross-section with places labelled: Shoreham, South Downs, Clay Vale, Greenwood Rdge, Southwater, [illegible], Cowfold, High Weald, North Downs, Guildford. Handwritten annotation on map: "we cycled 10k & 10k back" and printed caption "— Route of the Downs Link"]

It was a particularly pleasant bike ride because it didn't have demon [f]ast cyclists. They are out on the downs. There were quite a few families, an old lady & man with a dog in a push chair & even Batman & Robin. The cycle route was a railway line axed by Beeching. We could see Lancing College

[glued-in business card: The Steyning Tea Rooms / Open 7 days a week 10-6 / www.steyningtearooms.co.uk / 32 High Street / Steyning / West Sussex / 01903 810103 / Kim Cook / Proprietor / info@steyningtearooms.co.uk]

chapel in the distance. At Steyning we had a stupendous tea - rhubarb scones, rhubarb jam & then set off home, back to the van

We reached Capel about 6 & spent the evening at Sonja's flat. Sonja & Jenny gave us supper. Charles brought champagne to celebrate the end of Jenny's exams.
__Monday__
   We set off for Petworth. We shopped in the town for lunch tomorrow. (Un-pleasant deli-keeper) then looked at the house. Amazing paintings, wood carvings, kitchens. Helpful NT volunteers. Originally the house was the Duke of Northumberland's hunting lodge. When Queen Elizabeth called the Percys to live in the South, where she could keep an eye on them, they decided to develop the lodge into a home.
   We had lunch in the van & then walked in a part of the

	We tried to see a bit of Salmon's
Bridge Farm, the Campins home that
Simon visited when he was 10, then
continued on to Jane in Churt.
	We had supper & slept at
Longwood House
__Tuesday__
	Simon drove us to the factory,
Ash Vale & Jane & Eve caught the
train to go to the Light box
exhibition on Gertrude Jekyll 1843-1932.
We met up with Julie Aldam for 20
minutes. She pointed us on our walk
to Kate Ruby's House. Simon got there
before us. Kate was there with Dora
& Mylo who slept all the time.
	After lunch we drove Jane back
to Churt & went on to Surs [Jew]

captions: Tulips & Roses with tracings made preparatory to embroidery. Drawing by Gertrude Jekyll.
pages.text:
to admire the new flat that Charles' landlord is preparing for him. We had supper at the White Horse with Charles + Jenny + drove on home.

[photo/postcard glued in: Tulips & Roses with tracings made preparatory to embroidery. Drawing by Gertrude Jekyll.]
